questions.prompt: Complete: "Usually I take the bus, but yesterday I ___ a taxi."
questions.explanation: "Usually" points to a present habit (take), but "yesterday" points to a finished past action. "Take" is irregular, and its past is "took": yesterday I took a taxi ✓. "Taked" wrongly adds -ed, and "taken" is the past participle.

questions.prompt: Choose the sentence with the correct word order:

options.text: My friends play often football after school.
My friends football often play after school.
My friends often play football after school.
My friends play football often after school.

questions.explanation: An adverb of frequency goes before the main verb: my friends often play football ✓. "Play often football" and "play football often" put the adverb after the verb or its object, and "football often play" breaks the basic subject-verb-object order.

questions.prompt: Which is the correct past form of "buy"?
questions.explanation: "Buy" is irregular, and its simple past is "bought". "Buyed" and "buied" wrongly try to add -ed, and "boughted" adds -ed on top of the irregular form — the past is simply "bought" ✓.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct question for the answer: "I go swimming twice a week."

options.text: How often do you go swimming?
How many do you go swimming?
How long do you go swimming?

questions.explanation: "Twice a week" tells us how frequently, so the question asks "How often": How often do you go swimming? "How many" needs a noun (how many times), "How long" asks about duration, and "When" asks for a specific time, not a frequency.

questions.prompt: Complete: "We ___ the beginning of the film because we arrived late."
questions.explanation: The sentence is past and negative, so we use "didn't" + the base verb: didn't see ✓. The trap "didn't saw" keeps the past form after "did"; "didn't seen" uses the past participle; and "don't see" is present, which clashes with "arrived late".

questions.prompt: Read the text: "(1) My name is Skander. (2) I am thirteen years old. (3) Last year, I win the school chess tournament. (4) Now I play every weekend." Which sentence contains a mistake?
questions.explanation: This item asks you to find the faulty sentence. Sentence 3 says "Last year", a finished past time, but uses the present "win" — it must be the past "won": Last year, I won the tournament ✓. Sentences 1, 2 and 4 correctly use the present for facts and habits ("is", "am", "play every weekend").
